executive_summary: Conflict has escalated to direct US-Iran naval confrontation with IRGC claiming to launch 4 ballistic missiles at USS Abraham Lincoln. Iran has expanded strikes to target Dubai Airport and Sharjah, while Israeli airstrikes continue on Tehran with reports of 148 casualties at a girls school. The 24h incident count has risen to 603 (213 Major, 256 High). US has deployed B-2 stealth bombers against Iran. Three US service members confirmed killed in Operation Epic Fury — first US casualties.
whats_new: IRGC claims to have launched 4 ballistic missiles at USS Abraham Lincoln aircraft carrier near Iranian waters — direct attack on major US naval asset
Iranian missiles struck Dubai Airport and Sharjah causing fires — first direct attack on critical UAE infrastructure
Death toll from Israeli-US strike on Iranian girls school rises to 148 (from earlier report of 115)
US deployed four B-2 stealth bombers for strikes on Iran — escalation to strategic bombing assets
Two commercial ships struck by projectiles in Strait of Hormuz — escalating threat to global oil shipping
Unidentified drone crashed inside US base in Kuwait — vulnerability of US Gulf bases demonstrated
confirmed_events: Multi-source confirmation: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei assassinated in US-Israeli strikes on Tehran, along with at least 40 senior Iranian commanders and former President Mahmoud Ahmadinejad
Multi-source: Iran launched mass ballistic missile strikes across Israel, UAE, Bahrain, Jordan, and Oman; UAE reports 3 dead/58 injured, Kuwait 1 dead/32 injured
Multi-source: Israeli Air Force achieves air superiority over Iran within 24 hours; ongoing strikes on Tehran targeting leadership and military infrastructure
Confirmed by CENTCOM: Three US service members killed, five wounded in Operation Epic Fury — first US casualties in conflict
Multi-source: Ben Gurion Airport closed until Wednesday; UAE intercepted 152 of 165 missiles and 311 drones
unverified_emerging: IRGC claims to have struck USS Abraham Lincoln — status of carrier damage unconfirmed (sources: IRGC statements, not yet corroborated by US CENTCOM)
Israeli army denies knowledge of strike on Iranian school killing 148 — Israel has not confirmed involvement despite Iranian claims of US-Israeli action

people_travel: Ben Gurion Airport closed until at least Wednesday for evacuation flights
All commercial flights to/from Israel severely disrupted
Kuwait and UAE airspace under active missile defense operations
supply_chain: Strait of Hormuz: Two ships struck by projectiles — critical maritime chokepoint threatened
Dubai Airport and Sharjah impacted by Iranian missile strikes — cargo operations disrupted
Gulf port operations in UAE, Kuwait under missile threat — shipping delays expected
Global oil supply chain at risk if Strait of Hormuz operations disrupted further
market_macro: Oil prices likely to spike on Strait of Hormuz incidents and carrier attack claims
Flight to safety: Gold, US Treasury yields up; risk assets down
Potential energy supply shock if Gulf shipping disrupted — monitor Brent crude futures

outlook_24_72h.base_case: Conflict will intensify over next 24-72h. Iran has signaled continued 'Al-Wa'd Al-Sadeq 4' operations with seventh and eighth waves announced. Leadership succession process underway in Tehran but military operations continue under President Bzeshkian. Israel has achieved air superiority enabling sustained strikes. US carrier targeted — expect strong American retaliatory response. Regional proxies (Hezbollah, Iraqi militia) likely to increase attacks on US bases in Iraq, Syria, and Gulf states.
escalation_risks: USS Abraham Lincoln strike — if confirmed hit, major US retaliation expected
Strait of Hormuz closure would trigger global oil supply crisis
Iranian missiles targeting more Gulf critical infrastructure (oil terminals, desalination plants)
Hezbollah escalation from Lebanon against Israeli northern cities
US domestic political pressure for expanded military action after casualties
outlook_24_72h.de_escalation_pathways: De-escalation highly unlikely in near term. Iran has suffered catastrophic leadership losses and will likely continue strikes to demonstrate resolve. US and Israel appear committed to sustained campaign. Only potential off-ramp would be complete neutralization of Iranian military capacity or internal Iranian regime collapse — neither achievable in 72h window. Regional diplomatic efforts by Gulf states may provide backchannel but currently no formal ceasefire framework.
